content.content:
1	"""
2	Z-Wave JS lock provider.
3	
4	Handles push updates, duplicate code detection, and rate-limited set/clear operations.
5	See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider's role in the data flow.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from collections.abc import Callable
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from datetime import timedelta
13	import functools
14	import logging
15	from typing import Any, Literal
16	
17	from zwave_js_server.client import Client
18	from zwave_js_server.const import CommandClass, NodeStatus
19	from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.lock import (
20	    ATTR_CODE_SLOT,
21	    ATTR_IN_USE,
22	    ATTR_USERCODE,
23	    LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
24	    LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
25	    CodeSlotStatus,
26	)
27	from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.notification import (
28	    AccessControlNotificationEvent,
29	    NotificationType,
30	)
31	from zwave_js_server.exceptions import FailedZWaveCommand
32	from zwave_js_server.model.node import Node
33	from zwave_js_server.util.lock import (
34	    get_usercode,
35	    get_usercode_from_node,
36	    get_usercodes,
37	)
38	
39	from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.const import (
40	    ATTR_EVENT,
41	    ATTR_EVENT_LABEL,
42	    ATTR_HOME_ID,
43	    ATTR_NODE_ID,
44	    ATTR_PARAMETERS,
45	    ATTR_TYPE,
46	    DOMAIN as ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN,
47	    SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE,
48	    SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE,
49	    ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
50	)
51	from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.helpers import async_get_node_from_entity_id
52	from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.models import ZwaveJSData
53	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry, ConfigEntryState
54	from homeassistant.const import ATTR_DEVICE_ID, ATTR_ENTITY_ID
55	from homeassistant.core import Event, callback
56	
57	from ..domain.exceptions import LockDisconnected
58	from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
59	from ._base import BaseLock
60	
61	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
62	
63	# All known Access Control Notification CC events that indicate the lock is locked
64	# or unlocked
65	ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED = {
66	    True: (
67	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.AUTO_LOCK_LOCKED_OPERATION,
68	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_LOCK_OPERATION,
69	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
70	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCKED_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
71	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_LOCK_OPERATION,
72	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_LOCK_OPERATION,
73	    ),
74	    False: (
75	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
76	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
77	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
78	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
79	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
80	    ),
81	}
82	
83	
84	@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
85	class ZWaveJSLock(BaseLock):
86	    """Class to represent ZWave JS lock."""
87	
88	    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry = field(repr=False)
89	    # Home Assistant event-bus listeners (separate lifecycle from push
90	    # subscriptions: registered in ``async_setup``, released in
91	    # ``async_unload``).
92	    _listeners: list[Callable[[], None]] = field(init=False, default_factory=list)
93	    _set_in_progress_code_slot: int | None = field(init=False, default=None)
94	
95	    @property
96	    def node(self) -> Node:
97	        """Return ZWave JS node."""
98	        return async_get_node_from_entity_id(
99	            self.hass, self.lock.entity_id, self.ent_reg
100	        )
101	
102	    @functools.cached_property
103	    def _usercode_cc_version(self) -> int:
104	        """Return the User Code CC version supported by this node."""
105	        version = next(
106	            (
107	                cc.version
108	                for cc in self.node.command_classes
109	                if cc.id == CommandClass.USER_CODE
110	            ),
111	            0,
112	        )
113	        if version == 0:
114	            _LOGGER.warning(
115	                "Lock %s: User Code CC not found on node %s. This may "
116	                "indicate an incomplete interview. Defaulting to V1 behavior",
117	                self.lock.entity_id,
118	                self.node.node_id,
119	            )
120	            return 1
121	        return version
122	
123	    @property
124	    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
125	        """Return whether this lock supports push-based updates."""
126	        return True
127	
128	    @property
129	    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
130	        """Z-Wave JS exposes config entry state changes, so skip polling."""
131	        return None
132	
133	    def _get_client_state(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
134	        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is ready and a retry reason."""
135	        if self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
136	            return False, "config entry not loaded"
137	
138	        runtime_data: ZwaveJSData | None = getattr(
139	            self.lock_config_entry, "runtime_data", None
140	        )
141	        client: Client | None = (
142	            getattr(runtime_data, "client", None) if runtime_data else None
143	        )
144	        if not client:
145	            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not ready"
146	
147	        if not client.connected:
148	            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not connected"
149	
150	        if client.driver is None:
151	            return False, "Z-Wave JS driver not ready"
152	
153	        return True, ""
154	
155	    def code_slot_in_use(self, code_slot: int) -> bool | None:
156	        """Return whether a code slot is in use."""
157	        try:
158	            return get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)[ATTR_IN_USE]
159	        except KeyError, ValueError:
160	            return None
161	
162	    @callback
163	    def _handle_usercode_status_update(self, code_slot: int, status: Any) -> None:
164	        """Handle userIdStatus value update for a code slot."""
165	        if status == CodeSlotStatus.AVAILABLE:
166	            # Ignore AVAILABLE status if Lock Code Manager expects a PIN on this
167	            # slot. Some locks send stale AVAILABLE events after a code was set,
168	            # which would cause infinite sync loops.
169	            if (
170	                self.coordinator
171	                and self.coordinator.desired_credential(code_slot).is_present
172	            ):
173	                _LOGGER.debug(
174	                    "Lock %s: ignoring userIdStatus=AVAILABLE for slot %s "
175	                    "(LCM expects PIN on this slot)",
176	                    self.lock.entity_id,
177	                    code_slot,
178	                )
179	                return
180	
181	            # Slot was cleared - update coordinator if needed
182	            current = self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) if self.coordinator else None
183	            if self.coordinator and (current is None or not current.is_empty):
184	                _LOGGER.debug(
185	                    "Lock %s: slot %s userIdStatus=AVAILABLE, marking cleared",
186	                    self.lock.entity_id,
187	                    code_slot,
188	                )
189	                self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.empty())
190	
191	    @callback
192	    def _handle_usercode_value_update(self, code_slot: int, new_value: Any) -> None:
193	        """Handle userCode value update for a code slot."""
194	        if not new_value:
195	            resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
196	        else:
197	            value = str(new_value)
198	            slot_in_use = self.code_slot_in_use(code_slot)
199	            # Asymmetric in_use checks: masked codes count as unreadable even
200	            # when in_use is None (some firmwares mask before reporting
201	            # status), but all-zeros only counts as empty when in_use is
202	            # explicitly False (zeros from a partially-loaded cache must
203	            # not be misread as cleared).
204	            if value == "*" * len(value) and slot_in_use is not False:
205	                resolved = SlotCredential.unreadable()
206	            elif value.strip("0") == "" and slot_in_use is False:
207	                resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
208	            else:
209	                resolved = SlotCredential.known(value)
210	
211	        # Z-Wave JS sends duplicate events; skip if the value is unchanged.
212	        if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) == resolved:
213	            return
214	
215	        _LOGGER.debug(
216	            "Lock %s received push update for slot %s: %s",
217	            self.lock.entity_id,
218	            code_slot,
219	            "****" if resolved.is_readable else f"({resolved.as_label()})",
220	        )
221	        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, resolved)
222	
223	    @callback
224	    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
225	        """Subscribe to User Code CC value update events."""
226	        if self._push_unsubs:
227	            return
228	
229	        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()
230	        if not ready:
231	            raise LockDisconnected(reason)
232	
233	        @callback
234	        def on_value_updated(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
235	            """Handle value update events from Z-Wave JS."""
236	            args: dict[str, Any] = event["args"]
237	            if args.get("commandClass") != CommandClass.USER_CODE:
238	                return
239	
240	            property_name = args.get("property")
241	            if property_name not in (
242	                LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
243	                LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
244	            ):
245	                return
246	
247	            code_slot = int(args["propertyKey"])
248	
249	            # Slot 0 is not a valid user code slot.
250	            if code_slot == 0:
251	                return
252	
253	            # Clear in-progress tracking only on userCode updates for the slot
254	            # we were setting. userIdStatus updates don't confirm acceptance and
255	            # could race with duplicate-code notifications.
256	            if (
257	                property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY
258	                and code_slot == self._set_in_progress_code_slot
259	            ):
260	                self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
261	
262	            if property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY:
263	                self._handle_usercode_status_update(code_slot, args.get("newValue"))
264	            else:
265	                self._handle_usercode_value_update(code_slot, args.get("newValue"))
266	
267	        try:
268	            unsub = self.node.on("value updated", on_value_updated)
269	        except ValueError as err:
270	            raise LockDisconnected(f"node not ready: {err}") from err
271	        self._register_push_unsub(unsub)
272	
273	    @callback
274	    def teardown_push_subscription(self) -> None:
275	        """Unsubscribe from value update events."""
276	        self._clear_push_unsubs()
277	
278	    @callback
279	    def _zwave_js_event_filter(self, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
280	        """Return True if the event belongs to this lock's node."""
281	        assert self.node.client.driver
282	        return (
283	            event_data[ATTR_HOME_ID] == self.node.client.driver.controller.home_id
284	            and event_data[ATTR_NODE_ID] == self.node.node_id
285	            and event_data[ATTR_DEVICE_ID] == self.lock.device_id
286	        )
287	
288	    @callback
289	    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:
290	        """Handle Z-Wave JS event."""
291	        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:
292	            _LOGGER.debug(
293	                "Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s",
294	                self.lock.entity_id,
295	                evt.as_dict(),
296	            )
297	            return
298	
299	        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}
300	        code_slot = params.get("userId", 0)
301	
302	        # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set.
303	        # Mark the slot as rejected so the sync manager raises DuplicateCodeError
304	        # on the next tick, routing through the standard CodeRejectedError flow
305	        # (tracker reset, circuit breaker awareness, notification).
306	        # Some Z-Wave lock firmwares report this notification with userId=0
307	        # instead of the offending slot; treat 0 as referring to the slot
308	        # we're currently setting.
309	        if (
310	            evt.data[ATTR_EVENT]
311	            == AccessControlNotificationEvent.NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE
312	            and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None
313	            and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)
314	        ):
315	            slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot
316	            self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
317	            self.mark_code_rejected(slot)
318	            return
319	
320	        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
321	            code_slot=code_slot,
322	            to_locked=next(
323	                (
324	                    to_locked
325	                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()
326	                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes
327	                ),
328	                None,
329	            ),
330	            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),
331	            source_data=evt,
332	        )
333	
334	    @property
335	    def domain(self) -> str:
336	        """Return integration domain."""
337	        return ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN
338	
339	    def _clear_listeners(self) -> None:
340	        """Unsubscribe and clear all HA event bus listeners."""
341	        for listener in self._listeners:
342	            listener()
343	        self._listeners.clear()
344	
345	    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
346	        """
347	        Set up lock by provider.
348	
349	        Idempotent: clears existing listeners before re-registering.
350	        """
351	        self._clear_listeners()
352	        self._listeners.append(
353	            self.hass.bus.async_listen(
354	                ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
355	                self._handle_zwave_js_event,
356	                self._zwave_js_event_filter,
357	            )
358	        )
359	
360	    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
361	        """Unload lock."""
362	        self._clear_listeners()
363	        await super().async_unload(remove_permanently)
364	
365	    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
366	        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is connected."""
367	        ready, _reason = self._get_client_state()
368	        return ready
369	
370	    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
371	        """Return whether the Z-Wave node is available for commands."""
372	        try:
373	            return self.node.status != NodeStatus.DEAD
374	        except Exception as err:
375	            _LOGGER.debug(
376	                "Lock %s: failed to check device availability: %s",
377	                self.lock.entity_id,
378	                err,
379	            )
380	            return False
381	
382	    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
383	        """Refresh the User Code CC cache from the device and return all codes."""
384	        await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
385	        return await self.async_get_usercodes()
386	
387	    async def _async_verify_write(
388	        self, code_slot: int, operation: Literal["set", "clear"]
389	    ) -> None:
390	        """
391	        Force-update the value cache after a set/clear on a V1 lock.
392	
393	        V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after a write.
394	        Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache before
395	        the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops. Wrap failures as
396	        LockDisconnected so they route to the retry path instead of leaking a
397	        raw FailedZWaveCommand into the generic exception handler, which would
398	        otherwise suspend the lock.
399	        """
400	        if self._usercode_cc_version >= 2:
401	            return
402	        try:
403	            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
404	        except FailedZWaveCommand as err:
405	            raise LockDisconnected(
406	                f"Post-{operation} verification poll failed for "
407	                f"{self.lock.entity_id} slot {code_slot}: {err}"
408	            ) from err
409	
410	    async def async_set_usercode(
411	        self,
412	        code_slot: int,
413	        usercode: str,
414	        name: str | None = None,
415	        source: Literal["sync", "direct"] = "direct",
416	    ) -> bool:
417	        """
418	        Set a usercode on a code slot.
419	
420	        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.
421	        """
422	        # Cache lookup short-circuits no-op writes. Bare-except is intentional:
423	        # a stale or missing cache entry must not block the set operation.
424	        try:
425	            if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get("in_use"):
426	                current_code = str(current.get("usercode", ""))
427	                # Skip the duplicate check if the current code is masked.
428	                if current_code != "*" * len(current_code) and usercode == current_code:
429	                    _LOGGER.debug(
430	                        "Lock %s slot %s already has this PIN, skipping set",
431	                        self.lock.entity_id,
432	                        code_slot,
433	                    )
434	                    return False
435	        except Exception:
436	            pass
437	
438	        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot
439	        service_data = {
440	            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
441	            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
442	            ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,
443	        }
444	        await self.async_call_service(
445	            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
446	        )
447	        await self._async_verify_write(code_slot, "set")
448	        # Optimistic update: the value cache updates asynchronously via push
449	        # notification; push now to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache.
450	        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.known(usercode))
451	        return True
452	
453	    async def async_clear_usercode(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
454	        """
455	        Clear a usercode on a code slot.
456	
457	        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already cleared.
458	        """
459	        # Cache lookup short-circuits no-op clears. Bare-except is intentional:
460	        # see async_set_usercode for rationale.
461	        try:
462	            current = get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)
463	            if not current.get("in_use"):
464	                _LOGGER.debug(
465	                    "Lock %s slot %s already cleared, skipping clear",
466	                    self.lock.entity_id,
467	                    code_slot,
468	                )
469	                return False
470	        except Exception:
471	            pass
472	
473	        service_data = {
474	            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
475	            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
476	        }
477	        await self.async_call_service(
478	            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
479	        )
480	        await self._async_verify_write(code_slot, "clear")
481	        # Optimistic update: see async_set_usercode for rationale.
482	        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.empty())
483	        return True
484	
485	    def _get_usercodes_from_cache(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
486	        """Get usercodes from Z-Wave JS value DB cache."""
487	        try:
488	            return list(get_usercodes(self.node) or [])
489	        except Exception as err:
490	            raise LockDisconnected from err
491	
492	    async def _async_refresh_usercode_cache(self) -> None:
493	        """Refresh usercode cache from the device."""
494	        try:
495	            await self.node.async_refresh_cc_values(CommandClass.USER_CODE)
496	        except Exception as err:
497	            raise LockDisconnected from err
498	
499	    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
500	        """Get dictionary of code slots and usercodes."""
501	        code_slots = self.managed_slots
502	        data: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}
503	
504	        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
505	            raise LockDisconnected
506	
507	        slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
508	        slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}
509	
510	        # If any managed slot is missing or has unknown in_use state, do one hard
511	        # refresh. Call _async_refresh_usercode_cache directly (not
512	        # async_hard_refresh_codes) to avoid recursion.
513	        if any(
514	            slot_num not in slots_by_num or slots_by_num[slot_num].get("in_use") is None
515	            for slot_num in code_slots
516	        ):
517	            _LOGGER.debug(
518	                "Lock %s has missing/unknown slots, performing hard refresh",
519	                self.lock.entity_id,
520	            )
521	            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
522	            slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
523	            slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}
524	
525	        for slot in slots:
526	            code_slot = int(slot["code_slot"])
527	            usercode: str = slot["usercode"] or ""
528	            in_use: bool | None = slot["in_use"]
529	
530	            if not in_use:
531	                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.empty()
532	            elif not usercode:
533	                # in_use but no code content (cache partially populated); skip
534	                continue
535	            elif usercode == "*" * len(usercode):
536	                # Masked code (all asterisks with slot in use)
537	                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
538	            else:
539	                # Unmasked code
540	                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.known(usercode)
541	
542	        slots_with_pin = [s for s, v in data.items() if v.is_present]
543	        slots_empty = [s for s, v in data.items() if v.is_empty]
544	        _LOGGER.debug(

546	            self.lock.entity_id,

"""
Z-Wave JS lock provider.

Handles push updates, duplicate code detection, and rate-limited set/clear operations.
See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider's role in the data flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import timedelta
import functools
import logging
from typing import Any, Literal

from zwave_js_server.client import Client
from zwave_js_server.const import CommandClass, NodeStatus
from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.lock import (
    ATTR_CODE_SLOT,
    ATTR_IN_USE,
    ATTR_USERCODE,
    LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
    LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
    CodeSlotStatus,
)
from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.notification import (
    AccessControlNotificationEvent,
    NotificationType,
)
from zwave_js_server.exceptions import FailedZWaveCommand
from zwave_js_server.model.node import Node
from zwave_js_server.util.lock import (
    get_usercode,
    get_usercode_from_node,
    get_usercodes,
)

from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.const import (
    ATTR_EVENT,
    ATTR_EVENT_LABEL,
    ATTR_HOME_ID,
    ATTR_NODE_ID,
    ATTR_PARAMETERS,
    ATTR_TYPE,
    DOMAIN as ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN,
    SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE,
    SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE,
    ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
)
from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.helpers import async_get_node_from_entity_id
from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.models import ZwaveJSData
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry, ConfigEntryState
from homeassistant.const import ATTR_DEVICE_ID, ATTR_ENTITY_ID
from homeassistant.core import Event, callback

from ..domain.exceptions import LockDisconnected
from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
from ._base import BaseLock

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# All known Access Control Notification CC events that indicate the lock is locked
# or unlocked
ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED = {
    True: (
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.AUTO_LOCK_LOCKED_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_LOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCKED_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_LOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_LOCK_OPERATION,
    ),
    False: (
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
    ),
}


@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
class ZWaveJSLock(BaseLock):
    """Class to represent ZWave JS lock."""

    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry = field(repr=False)
    # Home Assistant event-bus listeners (separate lifecycle from push
    # subscriptions: registered in ``async_setup``, released in
    # ``async_unload``).
    _listeners: list[Callable[[], None]] = field(init=False, default_factory=list)
    _set_in_progress_code_slot: int | None = field(init=False, default=None)

    @property
    def node(self) -> Node:
        """Return ZWave JS node."""
        return async_get_node_from_entity_id(
            self.hass, self.lock.entity_id, self.ent_reg
        )

    @functools.cached_property
    def _usercode_cc_version(self) -> int:
        """Return the User Code CC version supported by this node."""
        version = next(
            (
                cc.version
                for cc in self.node.command_classes
                if cc.id == CommandClass.USER_CODE
            ),
            0,
        )
        if version == 0:
            _LOGGER.warning(
                "Lock %s: User Code CC not found on node %s. This may "
                "indicate an incomplete interview. Defaulting to V1 behavior",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                self.node.node_id,
            )
            return 1
        return version

    @property
    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether this lock supports push-based updates."""
        return True

    @property
    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
        """Z-Wave JS exposes config entry state changes, so skip polling."""
        return None

    def _get_client_state(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is ready and a retry reason."""
        if self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
            return False, "config entry not loaded"

        runtime_data: ZwaveJSData | None = getattr(
            self.lock_config_entry, "runtime_data", None
        )
        client: Client | None = (
            getattr(runtime_data, "client", None) if runtime_data else None
        )
        if not client:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not ready"

        if not client.connected:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not connected"

        if client.driver is None:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS driver not ready"

        return True, ""

    def code_slot_in_use(self, code_slot: int) -> bool | None:
        """Return whether a code slot is in use."""
        try:
            return get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)[ATTR_IN_USE]
        except KeyError, ValueError:
            return None

    @callback
    def _handle_usercode_status_update(self, code_slot: int, status: Any) -> None:
        """Handle userIdStatus value update for a code slot."""
        if status == CodeSlotStatus.AVAILABLE:
            # Ignore AVAILABLE status if Lock Code Manager expects a PIN on this
            # slot. Some locks send stale AVAILABLE events after a code was set,
            # which would cause infinite sync loops.
            if (
                self.coordinator
                and self.coordinator.desired_credential(code_slot).is_present
            ):
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s: ignoring userIdStatus=AVAILABLE for slot %s "
                    "(LCM expects PIN on this slot)",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    code_slot,
                )
                return

            # Slot was cleared - update coordinator if needed
            current = self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) if self.coordinator else None
            if self.coordinator and (current is None or not current.is_empty):
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s: slot %s userIdStatus=AVAILABLE, marking cleared",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    code_slot,
                )
                self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.empty())

    @callback
    def _handle_usercode_value_update(self, code_slot: int, new_value: Any) -> None:
        """Handle userCode value update for a code slot."""
        if not new_value:
            resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
        else:
            value = str(new_value)
            slot_in_use = self.code_slot_in_use(code_slot)
            # Asymmetric in_use checks: masked codes count as unreadable even
            # when in_use is None (some firmwares mask before reporting
            # status), but all-zeros only counts as empty when in_use is
            # explicitly False (zeros from a partially-loaded cache must
            # not be misread as cleared).
            if value == "*" * len(value) and slot_in_use is not False:
                resolved = SlotCredential.unreadable()
            elif value.strip("0") == "" and slot_in_use is False:
                resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
            else:
                resolved = SlotCredential.known(value)

        # Z-Wave JS sends duplicate events; skip if the value is unchanged.
        if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) == resolved:
            return

        _LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s received push update for slot %s: %s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            code_slot,
            "****" if resolved.is_readable else f"({resolved.as_label()})",
        )
        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, resolved)

    @callback
    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
        """Subscribe to User Code CC value update events."""
        if self._push_unsubs:
            return

        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()
        if not ready:
            raise LockDisconnected(reason)

        @callback
        def on_value_updated(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
            """Handle value update events from Z-Wave JS."""
            args: dict[str, Any] = event["args"]
            if args.get("commandClass") != CommandClass.USER_CODE:
                return

            property_name = args.get("property")
            if property_name not in (
                LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
                LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
            ):
                return

            code_slot = int(args["propertyKey"])

            # Slot 0 is not a valid user code slot.
            if code_slot == 0:
                return

            # Clear in-progress tracking only on userCode updates for the slot
            # we were setting. userIdStatus updates don't confirm acceptance and
            # could race with duplicate-code notifications.
            if (
                property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY
                and code_slot == self._set_in_progress_code_slot
            ):
                self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None

            if property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY:
                self._handle_usercode_status_update(code_slot, args.get("newValue"))
            else:
                self._handle_usercode_value_update(code_slot, args.get("newValue"))

        try:
            unsub = self.node.on("value updated", on_value_updated)
        except ValueError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(f"node not ready: {err}") from err
        self._register_push_unsub(unsub)

    @callback
    def teardown_push_subscription(self) -> None:
        """Unsubscribe from value update events."""
        self._clear_push_unsubs()

    @callback
    def _zwave_js_event_filter(self, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Return True if the event belongs to this lock's node."""
        assert self.node.client.driver
        return (
            event_data[ATTR_HOME_ID] == self.node.client.driver.controller.home_id
            and event_data[ATTR_NODE_ID] == self.node.node_id
            and event_data[ATTR_DEVICE_ID] == self.lock.device_id
        )

    @callback
    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:
        """Handle Z-Wave JS event."""
        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                evt.as_dict(),
            )
            return

        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}
        code_slot = params.get("userId", 0)

        # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set.
        # Mark the slot as rejected so the sync manager raises DuplicateCodeError
        # on the next tick, routing through the standard CodeRejectedError flow
        # (tracker reset, circuit breaker awareness, notification).
        # Some Z-Wave lock firmwares report this notification with userId=0
        # instead of the offending slot; treat 0 as referring to the slot
        # we're currently setting.
        if (
            evt.data[ATTR_EVENT]
            == AccessControlNotificationEvent.NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE
            and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None
            and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)
        ):
            slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot
            self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
            self.mark_code_rejected(slot)
            return

        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
            code_slot=code_slot,
            to_locked=next(
                (
                    to_locked
                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()
                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes
                ),
                None,
            ),
            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),
            source_data=evt,
        )

    @property
    def domain(self) -> str:
        """Return integration domain."""
        return ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN

    def _clear_listeners(self) -> None:
        """Unsubscribe and clear all HA event bus listeners."""
        for listener in self._listeners:
            listener()
        self._listeners.clear()

    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
        """
        Set up lock by provider.

        Idempotent: clears existing listeners before re-registering.
        """
        self._clear_listeners()
        self._listeners.append(
            self.hass.bus.async_listen(
                ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
                self._handle_zwave_js_event,
                self._zwave_js_event_filter,
            )
        )

    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
        """Unload lock."""
        self._clear_listeners()
        await super().async_unload(remove_permanently)

    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is connected."""
        ready, _reason = self._get_client_state()
        return ready

    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave node is available for commands."""
        try:
            return self.node.status != NodeStatus.DEAD
        except Exception as err:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: failed to check device availability: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return False

    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
        """Refresh the User Code CC cache from the device and return all codes."""
        await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
        return await self.async_get_usercodes()

    async def _async_verify_write(
        self, code_slot: int, operation: Literal["set", "clear"]
    ) -> None:
        """
        Force-update the value cache after a set/clear on a V1 lock.

        V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after a write.
        Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache before
        the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops. Wrap failures as
        LockDisconnected so they route to the retry path instead of leaking a
        raw FailedZWaveCommand into the generic exception handler, which would
        otherwise suspend the lock.
        """
        if self._usercode_cc_version >= 2:
            return
        try:
            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
        except FailedZWaveCommand as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Post-{operation} verification poll failed for "
                f"{self.lock.entity_id} slot {code_slot}: {err}"
            ) from err

    async def async_set_usercode(
        self,
        code_slot: int,
        usercode: str,
        name: str | None = None,
        source: Literal["sync", "direct"] = "direct",
    ) -> bool:
        """
        Set a usercode on a code slot.

        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.
        """
        # Cache lookup short-circuits no-op writes. Bare-except is intentional:
        # a stale or missing cache entry must not block the set operation.
        try:
            if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get("in_use"):
                current_code = str(current.get("usercode", ""))
                # Skip the duplicate check if the current code is masked.
                if current_code != "*" * len(current_code) and usercode == current_code:
                    _LOGGER.debug(
                        "Lock %s slot %s already has this PIN, skipping set",
                        self.lock.entity_id,
                        code_slot,
                    )
                    return False
        except Exception:
            pass

        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot
        service_data = {
            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
            ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,
        }
        await self.async_call_service(
            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
        )
        await self._async_verify_write(code_slot, "set")
        # Optimistic update: the value cache updates asynchronously via push
        # notification; push now to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache.
        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.known(usercode))
        return True

    async def async_clear_usercode(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
        """
        Clear a usercode on a code slot.

        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already cleared.
        """
        # Cache lookup short-circuits no-op clears. Bare-except is intentional:
        # see async_set_usercode for rationale.
        try:
            current = get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)
            if not current.get("in_use"):
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s slot %s already cleared, skipping clear",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    code_slot,
                )
                return False
        except Exception:
            pass

        service_data = {
            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
        }
        await self.async_call_service(
            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
        )
        await self._async_verify_write(code_slot, "clear")
        # Optimistic update: see async_set_usercode for rationale.
        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.empty())
        return True

    def _get_usercodes_from_cache(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get usercodes from Z-Wave JS value DB cache."""
        try:
            return list(get_usercodes(self.node) or [])
        except Exception as err:
            raise LockDisconnected from err

    async def _async_refresh_usercode_cache(self) -> None:
        """Refresh usercode cache from the device."""
        try:
            await self.node.async_refresh_cc_values(CommandClass.USER_CODE)
        except Exception as err:
            raise LockDisconnected from err

    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
        """Get dictionary of code slots and usercodes."""
        code_slots = self.managed_slots
        data: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}

        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
            raise LockDisconnected

        slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
        slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}

        # If any managed slot is missing or has unknown in_use state, do one hard
        # refresh. Call _async_refresh_usercode_cache directly (not
        # async_hard_refresh_codes) to avoid recursion.
        if any(
            slot_num not in slots_by_num or slots_by_num[slot_num].get("in_use") is None
            for slot_num in code_slots
        ):
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s has missing/unknown slots, performing hard refresh",
                self.lock.entity_id,
            )
            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
            slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
            slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}

        for slot in slots:
            code_slot = int(slot["code_slot"])
            usercode: str = slot["usercode"] or ""
            in_use: bool | None = slot["in_use"]

            if not in_use:
                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.empty()
            elif not usercode:
                # in_use but no code content (cache partially populated); skip
                continue
            elif usercode == "*" * len(usercode):
                # Masked code (all asterisks with slot in use)
                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
            else:
                # Unmasked code
                data[code_slot] = SlotCredential.known(usercode)

        slots_with_pin = [s for s, v in data.items() if v.is_present]
        slots_empty = [s for s, v in data.items() if v.is_empty]
        _LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: %s slots with PIN %s, %s slots empty %s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            len(slots_with_pin),
            slots_with_pin,
            len(slots_empty),
            slots_empty,
        )
        return data

1	"""
2	Z-Wave JS lock provider.
3	
4	Handles push updates via access-control credential node events and operation
5	notifications for lock/unlock state changes. See ARCHITECTURE.md for the
6	provider's role in the data flow.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations

11	from collections.abc import Callable
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field

18	from zwave_js_server.const import NodeStatus
19	from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.access_control import UserCredentialType
20	from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.notification import (

29	    ATTR_EVENT,
30	    ATTR_EVENT_LABEL,
31	    ATTR_HOME_ID,
32	    ATTR_NODE_ID,
33	    ATTR_PARAMETERS,
34	    ATTR_TYPE,
35	    DOMAIN as ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN,

37	)

39	from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.models import ZwaveJSData
40	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry, ConfigEntryState

45	from ..domain.credentials import (
46	    Credential,
47	    CredentialRef,
48	    CredentialType,
49	    CredentialTypeCapability,
50	    LockCapabilities,
51	    SetUserResult,
52	    User,
53	)
54	from ..domain.exceptions import (
55	    CodeRejectedError,
56	    DuplicateCodeError,
57	    LockDisconnected,
58	    LockOperationFailed,
59	)
60	from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
61	from ._util import parse_tag
62	from ._zwave_js_uc import ZWaveJSUserCodeFallbackSupport
63	
64	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
65	
66	# String key used by lock_helpers for Personal Identification Number credentials
67	# in the supported_credential_types dict returned by async_get_credential_capabilities.
68	_PIN_TYPE_STR = lock_helpers.CREDENTIAL_TYPE_MAP[UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE]
69	
70	# Z-Wave UserCredentialType -> domain CredentialType. The domain vocabulary
71	# is intentionally narrower than the Z-Wave one: types with no domain
72	# equivalent (BLE, UWB, DESFIRE, unspecified/eye/hand biometrics) are
73	# omitted and silently dropped by async_get_users. The base orchestration
74	# only acts on Personal Identification Number credentials today, but the
75	# non-PIN types we can represent are surfaced so direct callers (and a
76	# future expansion past PIN-only) see the full picture without another
77	# read.
78	_ZWAVE_TO_DOMAIN_CREDENTIAL_TYPE: dict[UserCredentialType, CredentialType] = {
79	    UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE: CredentialType.PIN,
80	    UserCredentialType.PASSWORD: CredentialType.PASSWORD,
81	    UserCredentialType.RFID_CODE: CredentialType.RFID,
82	    UserCredentialType.NFC: CredentialType.NFC,
83	    UserCredentialType.FACE_BIOMETRIC: CredentialType.FACE,
84	    UserCredentialType.FINGER_BIOMETRIC: CredentialType.FINGERPRINT,
85	}
86	
87	
88	# All known Access Control Notification CC events that indicate the lock is locked
89	# or unlocked
90	ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED = {
91	    True: (
92	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.AUTO_LOCK_LOCKED_OPERATION,
93	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_LOCK_OPERATION,
94	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
95	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCKED_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
96	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_LOCK_OPERATION,
97	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_LOCK_OPERATION,
98	    ),
99	    False: (
100	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
101	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
102	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
103	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
104	        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
105	    ),
106	}
107	
108	
109	@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
110	class ZWaveJSLock(ZWaveJSUserCodeFallbackSupport):
111	    """
112	    Class to represent ZWave JS lock.
113	
114	    Extends the temporary ``ZWaveJSUserCodeFallbackSupport`` layer (see
115	    ``_zwave_js_uc.py`` for the removal recipe once the upstream driver
116	    fix ships); this class holds only the unified-API implementation and
117	    the fallback routing branch points.
118	    """
119	
120	    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry = field(repr=False)
121	    # Home Assistant event-bus listeners (separate lifecycle from push
122	    # subscriptions: registered in ``async_setup``, released in
123	    # ``async_unload``).
124	    _listeners: list[Callable[[], None]] = field(init=False, default_factory=list)
125	
126	    @property
127	    def node(self) -> Node:
128	        """Return ZWave JS node."""
129	        return async_get_node_from_entity_id(
130	            self.hass, self.lock.entity_id, self.ent_reg
131	        )
132	
133	    @property
134	    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
135	        """Return whether this lock supports push-based updates."""
136	        return True
137	
138	    @property
139	    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
140	        """Z-Wave JS exposes config entry state changes, so skip polling."""
141	        return None
142	
143	    @property
144	    def hard_refresh_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
145	        """
146	        Re-read all credentials hourly to recover from missed push events.
147	
148	        Credentials are normally kept current by the access-control node-event
149	        push, but a missed or value-less event would otherwise strand a slot
150	        (for example as unreadable). This periodic drift refresh is the backstop.
151	        """
152	        return timedelta(hours=1)
153	
154	    @property
155	    def supports_native_users(self) -> bool:
156	        """Return True: this provider implements the credential primitives."""
157	        return True
158	
159	    def _pin_state(self, data: str | bytes | None) -> SlotCredential:
160	        """Project Z-Wave credential data to a SlotCredential (readable when present)."""
161	        if not data:
162	            return SlotCredential.unreadable()
163	        # CredentialData.data is str | bytes; decode bytes so a Personal
164	        # Identification Number is the digit string, not "b'1234'".
165	        return SlotCredential.known(data if isinstance(data, str) else data.decode())
166	
167	    async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
168	        """
169	        Read every user and all of their credentials from the lock.
170	
171	        Returns users carrying every credential type the domain model can
172	        represent (Personal Identification Number, Radio Frequency
173	        Identification, Near Field Communication, password, face,
174	        fingerprint). The base orchestration filters to Personal
175	        Identification Number at the slot-projection layer via
176	        ``user.pin_credentials``, so this method does no type-specific
177	        filtering -- direct callers see the full picture without an
178	        extra read. Z-Wave credential types with no domain equivalent
179	        (BLE, UWB, DESFIRE, unspecified/eye/hand biometrics) are
180	        dropped.
181	
182	        Uses the unified ``access_control`` API which dispatches to UC
183	        or U3C internally per node-zwave-js v15.23.4+. When that API is
184	        unusable for this lock (see ``async_get_capabilities``), users
185	        are synthesized from the User Code CC value DB instead: one
186	        implicit user per occupied slot with ``user_id == slot``,
187	        matching the User Code CC model where the user IS the credential.
188	        """
189	        if await self._async_uc_fallback_active():
190	            return await self._async_uc_users_from_value_db()
191	        try:
192	            users = await self.node.access_control.get_users_cached()
193	            credentials = await self.node.access_control.get_all_credentials_cached()
194	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
195	            raise LockDisconnected(f"get users failed: {err}") from err
196	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
197	            raise LockOperationFailed(f"get users failed: {err}") from err
198	        users_by_id: dict[int, User] = {
199	            user.user_id: User(
200	                user_id=user.user_id,
201	                name=user.user_name,
202	                active=user.active,
203	            )
204	            for user in users
205	        }
206	        for cred in credentials:
207	            domain_type = _ZWAVE_TO_DOMAIN_CREDENTIAL_TYPE.get(cred.type)
208	            owner = users_by_id.get(cred.user_id)
209	            if domain_type is None or owner is None:
210	                continue
211	            # _pin_state decodes the Personal Identification Number value
212	            # so the slot-projection sees a comparable string. For other
213	            # credential types the data is opaque to Lock Code Manager
214	            # (an RFID tag identifier, a biometric hash, ...), so they
215	            # surface as unreadable -- "the slot is occupied" without
216	            # revealing a value the integration would not act on anyway.
217	            state = (
218	                self._pin_state(cred.data)
219	                if cred.type is UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE
220	                else SlotCredential.unreadable()
221	            )
222	            owner.credentials.append(
223	                Credential(type=domain_type, slot=cred.slot, state=state)
224	            )
225	        return list(users_by_id.values())
226	
227	    async def async_get_capabilities(self) -> LockCapabilities:
228	        """
229	        Report the lock's user/credential capabilities.
230	
231	        Auto-detects User Code CC (UC) vs User Credential CC (U3C) and
232	        returns capabilities shaped so the seam routes through the right
233	        code path.
234	
235	        The unified ``access_control`` API in node-zwave-js computes its
236	        capabilities from cached interview data; on some locks that data
237	        is degenerate -- the PIN credential type comes back either
238	        missing or advertising ``num_slots=0`` even though the lock
239	        manages codes fine through the legacy User Code CC (issue
240	        #1251, upstream fix in zwave-js/zwave-js#8873). When that
241	        happens AND the node actually advertises User Code CC, we fall
242	        back to reading the lock's UC slot count from the value DB and
243	        return slot-only capabilities: ``supports_user_management=False``
244	        and ``max_user_name_length=0``, which the seam recognizes as a
245	        slot-only lock and routes through the credential-only
246	        primitives (``async_set_credential`` / ``async_delete_credential``
247	        / ``async_get_users``) without the user lifecycle. All
248	        credential operations then use the User Code CC utilities
249	        directly. Once the upstream fix ships and the unified API
250	        reports usable PIN capabilities for these locks, the fallback
251	        detection stops triggering on its own.
252	        """
253	        try:
254	            caps = await lock_helpers.async_get_credential_capabilities(self.node)
255	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
256	            raise LockDisconnected(f"get capabilities failed: {err}") from err
257	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
258	            raise LockOperationFailed(f"get capabilities failed: {err}") from err
259	        pin = caps["supported_credential_types"].get(_PIN_TYPE_STR)
260	
261	        if pin and pin["num_slots"] > 0:
262	            # The unified API advertises real PIN credential slots.
263	            self._uc_fallback = False
264	            return LockCapabilities(
265	                supports_user_management=caps["supports_user_management"],
266	                max_users=caps["max_users"],
267	                credential_types={
268	                    CredentialType.PIN: CredentialTypeCapability(
269	                        num_slots=pin["num_slots"],
270	                        min_length=pin["min_length"],
271	                        max_length=pin["max_length"],
272	                        supports_learn=pin["supports_learn"],
273	                    )
274	                },
275	                max_user_name_length=caps.get("max_user_name_length", 0),
276	            )
277	
278	        # Degenerate unified capabilities (issue #1251): try the User Code
279	        # CC fallback layer; when it does not apply either, the lock has
280	        # no PIN support LCM can manage.
281	        if uc_caps := self._uc_fallback_capabilities():
282	            return uc_caps
283	        return LockCapabilities(
284	            supports_user_management=False,
285	            max_users=0,
286	            credential_types={},
287	            max_user_name_length=0,
288	        )
289	
290	    async def async_set_user(self, user: User) -> SetUserResult:
291	        """
292	        Find-or-create the lock user for the LCM slot encoded in ``user.name``.
293	
294	        The base seam passes a tagged ``user.name`` (``lcm:<slot>:<display>``)
295	        whose slot is the LCM-side identity for this credential. The Z-Wave
296	        lock's own ``user_id`` is whatever Z-Wave happens to allocate; LCM
297	        no longer pins it to the slot. Discovery on every call:
298	
299	        1. Scan the lock's current user list for a user whose name carries
300	           the same ``lcm:<slot>:`` tag.
301	        2. If found (UPDATE): rename via ``async_set_user`` with the
302	           existing ``user_id``, return that id.
303	        3. If not (legacy adoption): scan for an *untagged* user whose
304	           ``user_id == slot`` that also owns a PIN at ``credential.slot
305	           == slot``. Pre-PR-C LCM pinned ``user_id`` to the slot, so
306	           such a user is almost certainly the LCM 2.0 user for this
307	           slot. Adopting it preserves a single per-slot anchor across
308	           the upgrade.
309	        4. Otherwise (CREATE): allocate a fresh ``user_id`` via
310	           ``async_set_user(user_id=None)`` (Z-Wave finds first free).
311	
312	        ``user.user_id`` set by the seam is used only as the slot identity
313	        when ``user.name`` is untagged (defensive fallback; the seam
314	        always passes a tagged name on this code path).
315	        """
316	        slot = self._slot_from_seam_user(user)
317	        existing_user_id = await self._find_user_index_for_slot(slot)
318	        write_user_id: int | None = existing_user_id
319	        try:
320	            result = await lock_helpers.async_set_user(
321	                self.node,
322	                user_id=write_user_id,
323	                user_name=user.name,
324	                active=user.active,
325	            )
326	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
327	            raise LockDisconnected(f"set user for slot {slot} failed: {err}") from err
328	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
329	            raise LockOperationFailed(
330	                f"set user for slot {slot} failed: {err}"
331	            ) from err
332	        return SetUserResult(
333	            user_id=result["user_id"], created=existing_user_id is None
334	        )
335	
336	    def _slot_from_seam_user(self, user: User) -> int:
337	        """
338	        Return the LCM slot encoded in ``user.name`` or fall back to ``user_id``.
339	
340	        The base seam always passes a tagged name; this helper centralizes
341	        the fallback so the rest of the provider can treat the resolved
342	        slot as a single value.
343	        """
344	        if user.name:
345	            slot, _ = parse_tag(user.name)
346	            if slot is not None:
347	                return slot
348	        return user.user_id
349	
350	    async def _find_user_index_for_slot(self, slot: int) -> int | None:
351	        """
352	        Return the lock ``user_id`` LCM owns for ``slot``, if any.
353	
354	        Two lookups, in priority order:
355	
356	        1. **Canonical** -- a user whose name carries the ``lcm:<slot>:``
357	           tag. This is the post-PR-C identity rule.
358	        2. **Legacy adoption** -- an *untagged* user whose ``user_id ==
359	           slot`` AND who owns a Personal Identification Number
360	           credential at ``credential.slot == slot``. Pre-PR-C LCM pinned
361	           ``user_id`` to the slot, so a user matching both halves of the
362	           old invariant is almost certainly the LCM 2.0 user for this
363	           slot. Adopting it (the subsequent ``async_set_user`` rewrites
364	           the name to the tagged form) preserves a single identifiable
365	           user per slot across the upgrade. Without this fallback the
366	           new model would CREATE a second user every time, silently
367	           leaving the pre-upgrade Personal Identification Number active
368	           on the lock.
369	
370	           The legacy pass MUST skip users whose names already parse to
371	           ANY LCM slot, so a user tagged for slot A is never adopted as
372	           slot B's anchor.
373	
374	        Returns ``None`` when neither lookup matches.
375	        """
376	        users = await self.async_get_users()
377	        try:
378	            return next(
379	                existing.user_id
380	                for existing in users
381	                if existing.name and parse_tag(existing.name)[0] == slot
382	            )
383	        except StopIteration:
384	            return next(
385	                (
386	                    existing.user_id
387	                    for existing in users
388	                    if existing.user_id == slot
389	                    and parse_tag(existing.name or "")[0] is None
390	                    for cred in existing.pin_credentials
391	                    if cred.slot == slot
392	                ),
393	                None,
394	            )
395	
396	    async def async_delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
397	        """Delete the lock user (cascades its credentials)."""
398	        try:
399	            await lock_helpers.async_delete_user(self.node, user_id)
400	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
401	            raise LockDisconnected(f"delete user {user_id} failed: {err}") from err
402	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
403	            raise LockOperationFailed(f"delete user {user_id} failed: {err}") from err
404	
405	    async def async_set_credential(
406	        self,
407	        user_id: int,
408	        credential: Credential,
409	        pin: str,
410	        *,
411	        name: str | None,
412	        source: Literal["sync", "direct"],
413	    ) -> bool:
414	        """
415	        Write the PIN credential under user_id; map device rejections.
416	
417	        In UC-fallback mode the write goes through the legacy User Code
418	        CC utilities (``set_usercode``), which address the slot directly
419	        and never consult the unified API's broken capability data.
420	        Otherwise the write goes through HA's
421	        ``lock_helpers.async_set_credential``, whose translation-key
422	        errors are mapped to LCM's typed exceptions.
423	        """
424	        if await self._async_uc_fallback_active():
425	            return await self._async_uc_set_usercode(credential.slot, pin)
426	        try:
427	            await lock_helpers.async_set_credential(
428	                self.node,
429	                user_id,
430	                UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE,
431	                pin,
432	                credential_slot=credential.slot,
433	            )
434	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
435	            # Transient Z-Wave command failure (e.g. a sleeping/battery lock):
436	            # route to retry rather than slot suspension.
437	            raise LockDisconnected(
438	                f"set credential slot {credential.slot} failed: {err}"
439	            ) from err
440	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
441	            if getattr(err, "translation_key", None) == "credential_rejected_duplicate":
442	                raise DuplicateCodeError(
443	                    code_slot=credential.slot,
444	                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
445	                ) from err
446	            raise CodeRejectedError(
447	                code_slot=credential.slot,
448	                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
449	                reason=str(err),
450	            ) from err
451	        return True
452	
453	    async def async_delete_credential(self, ref: CredentialRef) -> bool:
454	        """
455	        Delete the credential addressed by ref.
456	
457	        In UC-fallback mode the clear goes through the legacy User Code
458	        CC utilities (``clear_usercode``); otherwise through HA's
459	        ``lock_helpers.async_delete_credential``.
460	        """
461	        if await self._async_uc_fallback_active():
462	            return await self._async_uc_clear_usercode(ref.slot)
463	        try:
464	            await lock_helpers.async_delete_credential(
465	                self.node, ref.user_id, UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE, ref.slot
466	            )
467	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
468	            raise LockDisconnected(
469	                f"delete credential slot {ref.slot} failed: {err}"
470	            ) from err
471	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
472	            raise LockOperationFailed(
473	                f"delete credential slot {ref.slot} failed: {err}"
474	            ) from err
475	        return True
476	
477	    def _get_client_state(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
478	        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is ready and a retry reason."""
479	        if self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
480	            return False, "config entry not loaded"
481	
482	        runtime_data: ZwaveJSData | None = getattr(
483	            self.lock_config_entry, "runtime_data", None
484	        )
485	        client: Client | None = (
486	            getattr(runtime_data, "client", None) if runtime_data else None
487	        )
488	        if not client:
489	            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not ready"
490	
491	        if not client.connected:
492	            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not connected"
493	
494	        if client.driver is None:
495	            return False, "Z-Wave JS driver not ready"
496	
497	        return True, ""
498	
499	    @callback
500	    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
501	        """
502	        Subscribe to credential change events.
503	
504	        In unified mode the driver emits ``credential added/modified/
505	        deleted`` node events. In UC-fallback mode those events never
506	        fire (the driver only emits them from its own unified API
507	        methods, which the fallback bypasses), so we subscribe to raw
508	        ``value updated`` events for the User Code CC values instead --
509	        the same push source the legacy 3.x provider used. When the
510	        mode is not yet known (capability probe hasn't run), subscribe
511	        to both; the handlers are self-filtering and pushes are
512	        idempotent.
513	        """
514	        if self._push_unsubs:
515	            return
516	
517	        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()
518	        if not ready:
519	            raise LockDisconnected(reason)
520	
521	        subscriptions: list[tuple[str, Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None]]] = []
522	        if self._uc_fallback is not False:
523	            subscriptions.append(("value updated", self._on_uc_value_updated))
524	        if not self._uc_fallback:
525	            subscriptions.extend(
526	                (
527	                    ("credential added", self._on_credential_changed),
528	                    ("credential modified", self._on_credential_changed),
529	                    ("credential deleted", self._on_credential_deleted),
530	                )
531	            )
532	
533	        try:
534	            for name, handler in subscriptions:
535	                self._register_push_unsub(self.node.on(name, handler))
536	        except ValueError as err:
537	            self._clear_push_unsubs()
538	            raise LockDisconnected(f"node not ready: {err}") from err
539	
540	    @callback
541	    def _on_credential_changed(self, event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
542	        """
543	        Handle credential added/modified events from the node.
544	
545	        The event carries the value when the lock includes it (e.g. an
546	        out-of-band keypad change), so push the readable state rather
547	        than always unreadable -- otherwise the slot would be stranded
548	        as unreadable until the next set/clear or hard refresh.
549	        """
550	        args = event["args"]  # CredentialChangedArgs (pre-parsed by the library)
551	        if args.credential_type != UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE:
552	            return
553	        self._push_credential_update(args.credential_slot, self._pin_state(args.data))
554	
555	    @callback
556	    def _on_credential_deleted(self, event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
557	        """Handle credential deleted events from the node."""
558	        args = event["args"]  # CredentialDeletedArgs (pre-parsed by the library)
559	        if args.credential_type != UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE:
560	            return
561	        self._push_credential_update(args.credential_slot, SlotCredential.empty())
562	
563	    @callback
564	    def teardown_push_subscription(self) -> None:
565	        """Unsubscribe from credential change events."""
566	        self._clear_push_unsubs()
567	
568	    @callback
569	    def _zwave_js_event_filter(self, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
570	        """Return True if the event belongs to this lock's node."""
571	        assert self.node.client.driver
572	        return (
573	            event_data[ATTR_HOME_ID] == self.node.client.driver.controller.home_id
574	            and event_data[ATTR_NODE_ID] == self.node.node_id
575	            and event_data[ATTR_DEVICE_ID] == self.lock.device_id
576	        )
577	
578	    @callback
579	    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:
580	        """Handle Z-Wave JS event."""
581	        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:
582	            _LOGGER.debug(
583	                "Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s",
584	                self.lock.entity_id,
585	                evt.as_dict(),
586	            )
587	            return
588	
589	        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}
590	        code_slot = params.get("userId", 0)
591	
592	        if self._uc_handle_duplicate_notification(evt, code_slot):
593	            return
594	
595	        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
596	            code_slot=code_slot,
597	            to_locked=next(
598	                (
599	                    to_locked
600	                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()
601	                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes
602	                ),
603	                None,
604	            ),
605	            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),
606	            source_data=evt,
607	        )
608	
609	    @property
610	    def domain(self) -> str:
611	        """Return integration domain."""
612	        return ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN
613	
614	    def _clear_listeners(self) -> None:
615	        """Unsubscribe and clear all HA event bus listeners."""
616	        for listener in self._listeners:
617	            listener()
618	        self._listeners.clear()
619	
620	    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
621	        """
622	        Set up lock by provider.
623	
624	        Idempotent: clears existing listeners before re-registering.
625	        Capability validation runs in the base ``async_setup_internal``.
626	        """
627	        self._clear_listeners()
628	        self._listeners.append(
629	            self.hass.bus.async_listen(
630	                ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
631	                self._handle_zwave_js_event,
632	                self._zwave_js_event_filter,
633	            )
634	        )
635	
636	    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
637	        """Unload lock."""
638	        self._clear_listeners()
639	        await super().async_unload(remove_permanently)
640	
641	    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
642	        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is connected."""
643	        ready, _reason = self._get_client_state()
644	        return ready
645	
646	    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
647	        """Return whether the Z-Wave node is available for commands."""
648	        try:
649	            return self.node.status != NodeStatus.DEAD
650	        except Exception as err:
651	            _LOGGER.debug(
652	                "Lock %s: failed to check device availability: %s",
653	                self.lock.entity_id,
654	                err,
655	            )
656	            return False
657	
658	    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
659	        """Re-read users AND credentials fresh from the device, then project to slots."""
660	        if await self._async_uc_fallback_active():
661	            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
662	            return await self.async_get_usercodes()
663	        try:
664	            await self.node.access_control.get_users()
665	            await self.node.access_control.get_all_credentials()
666	        except BaseZwaveJSServerError as err:
667	            raise LockDisconnected(f"hard refresh failed: {err}") from err
668	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
669	            raise LockOperationFailed(f"hard refresh failed: {err}") from err
670	        return await self.async_get_usercodes()
671
